title: cmate-worktree-setup result v1
description: Structured result returned by the cmate-worktree-setup Skill. Closed schema: an unknown field is a contract violation, not something a reader may ignore. No absolute machine path, token or secret ever appears here; the setup either created every planned worktree or it says which phase it stopped at and why.

status.description: success: every requested worktree was created and its baseline passed. partial: at least one worktree was created but a baseline failed, sync was unavailable, or an issue was skipped for collision; created worktrees are retained. failure: no worktree was created.

phase_reached.description: The furthest phase the run reached. A failure at inspect or plan means nothing was created.

issue_numbers.description: Positive integers only. A non-integer or a value below 1 is rejected before any inspection.

max_issues.description: Upper bound on how many issues one run may set up. issue_numbers longer than this is truncated and the drop is recorded in limitations.

profile_override.description: The profile the caller pinned, or null to let detection decide.

base_override.description: A base ref the caller pinned instead of the profile default. Recorded, never a machine path.

reuse_existing.description: True only when the caller explicitly asked to reuse an exact-match branch/directory/worktree. Never inferred.

slug.description: owner/name derived from the remote. null when no remote resolves. The repository root is never reported as an absolute path.

integration_branch.description: The branch worktree work integrates back into, resolved from the profile, not hardcoded.

dirty.description: Whether the integration worktree had uncommitted changes. A dirty integration worktree is never modified.

selected.description: node = Node/CommandMate, rust = Rust/CommandAgent. unverified = detected neither cleanly; used only after the caller confirms it before the run.

verified.description: True only for node and rust, the two profiles with recorded behaviour. Always false for unverified.

detection_evidence.description: The repository-relative files that decided the profile. Empty allows only the unverified selection.

baseline_command.description: The proportional baseline the profile prescribes. Never hardcoded to npm or cargo; resolved from the profile.

plan.description: The dry-run plan per issue, computed before anything is created. Empty only when the run failed at inspect.

worktrees.description: One entry per worktree the run acted on. created=false with reused=false means it was planned but not made.

available.description: Whether a CommandMate worktree-sync path exists in this environment. Absence is not a failure: sync is optional.

worktree_id.description: The CommandMate worktree id when sync ran and returned one. null when sync was unavailable or did not return an id.

collisions.description: Every existing branch, directory or worktree that matched a planned target. A collision blocks creation unless reuse_existing was set for an exact match.

redactions.description: Kind and count only. The redacted value never appears, here or anywhere else in the document.

blocking_reasons.description: Why the run could not proceed. Non-empty whenever status is failure.

limitations.description: Capabilities that were unavailable and the fallback taken, plus any dropped issues.

git_sha_nullable.description: A resolved commit SHA, or null before one was resolved. A symbolic ref alone is never enough to record a base.

repo_path.description: Repository-relative POSIX path. No leading slash, no backslash, no control character and no '..' segment: a result must not be able to name anything outside the repository or leak a machine-absolute path.

kind.description: What already exists. worktree is decided from `git worktree list --porcelain`, never from a substring grep.

detail.description: The existing name, redacted of any absolute path.

base_sha.description: The base resolved to a commit SHA at plan time. The branch is created from this SHA, not from the symbolic ref alone.

sync_planned.description: Whether CommandMate sync is intended for this worktree. False when sync is unavailable.

blocked_by.description: Collision kinds that block creating this entry. Empty means the entry is clear to create.

base_sha.description: The SHA re-confirmed immediately before creation. null when the entry was not created.

reused.description: True only when reuse_existing was set and an exact-match target was reused. created and reused are never both true.

outcome.description: Measured, never rounded. A failed baseline is fail, and the worktree that produced it is retained for diagnosis.

exit_code.description: The measured exit code. null when the command was killed, timed out or never ran; never write 0 in that case.

redacted.description: Whether output_excerpt had secrets or absolute paths removed before it was recorded.

output_excerpt.description: A short, redacted tail of the output for diagnosis. The full raw terminal stream is never stored.
